def truncate_headline(text, limit):
    if len(text) <= limit:
        return text
    # The ellipsis character '…' is 1 character
    # So the truncated text can be at most (limit - 1) chars before the ellipsis
    available = limit - 1
    if available <= 0:
        return '…'
    # Find the last word boundary within available chars
    truncated = text[:available]
    last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')
    if last_space == -1:
        # No space found, cut at available chars
        return truncated + '…'
    return truncated[:last_space] + '…'

def truncate_headline(text, limit):
    if len(text) <= limit:
        return text
    # We need to fit within limit chars including the ellipsis character '…'
    # '…' is a single character, so we have limit-1 chars for the text portion
    available = limit - 1  # 1 char for '…'
    truncated = text[:available]
    # If the character right after our cut is a space or we're at the end,
    # the cut is already on a word boundary. Otherwise find the last space.
    if len(text) > available and text[available] != ' ':
        last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')
        if last_space > 0:
            truncated = truncated[:last_space]
    return truncated + '…'

def truncate_headline(text, limit):
    """
    Shortens a headline to fit within `limit` characters, cutting on a word
    boundary and ending with a single ellipsis character '…'.
    If the text already fits within `limit`, return it unchanged.
    """
    if len(text) <= limit:
        return text

    # '…' is a single character, so we have (limit - 1) chars for the text portion.
    available = limit - 1  # 1 char for ellipsis

    if available <= 0:
        # Edge case: limit is 0 or 1, can only return ellipsis or nothing
        return '…'[:limit]

    # Cut at available chars
    truncated = text[:available]

    # Check if we're already at a word boundary:
    # If the next character after our cut is a space (or we're at the end),
    # then truncated ends at a full word.
    next_char_is_boundary = (available >= len(text) or text[available] in ' \t\n')

    if next_char_is_boundary:
        # Already at word boundary, strip trailing spaces and add ellipsis
        return truncated.rstrip() + '…'
    else:
        # We're mid-word, find the last space to cut cleanly
        last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')
        if last_space == -1:
            # No space found, just cut at available (best we can do)
            return truncated + '…'
        else:
            return truncated[:last_space] + '…'

def truncate_headline(text, limit):
    """Shortens a headline to fit within limit characters.

    Cuts on a word boundary and ends with a single ellipsis character '…'.
    If the text already fits within limit, returns it unchanged.
    """
    if len(text) <= limit:
        return text

    # We need to fit within limit chars including the ellipsis '…'
    # '…' is a single character, so we have limit-1 chars for the text portion
    available = limit - 1  # reserve 1 char for '…'

    # Truncate at available chars and find the last word boundary
    truncated = text[:available]

    # Find the last space to cut on a word boundary
    last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')

    if last_space == -1:
        # No space found, hard cut at available chars
        return truncated + '…'

    # Cut at the last space (don't include trailing space)
    return truncated[:last_space] + '…'

def truncate_headline(text, limit):
    """Shorten a headline to fit within limit characters.

    Cuts on a word boundary and appends a single ellipsis character '…'.
    If the text already fits within limit, returns it unchanged.
    """
    if len(text) <= limit:
        return text
    # Reserve one character for the ellipsis
    available = limit - 1
    truncated = text[:available]
    last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')
    if last_space > 0:
        truncated = truncated[:last_space]
    return truncated + '…'

def truncate_headline(text, limit):
    """Shorten a headline to fit within limit characters.

    Cuts on a word boundary and ends with ellipsis '…'.
    If the text already fits within limit, returns it unchanged.
    """
    if len(text) <= limit:
        return text

    # Reserve 1 character for the ellipsis '…'
    available = limit - 1

    if available <= 0:
        return '…'

    truncated = text[:available]

    # Find the last space to cut on a word boundary
    last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')

    if last_space > 0:
        truncated = truncated[:last_space].rstrip()
    # If no space found, cut mid-word (unavoidable at very tight limits)

    return truncated + '…'

def truncate_headline(text, limit):
    """
    Shortens a headline to fit within `limit` characters, cutting on a word
    boundary and ending with a single ellipsis character '…'.
    If the text already fits within `limit`, return it unchanged.
    """
    if len(text) <= limit:
        return text

    # We need to fit: some words + '…' within `limit` characters.
    # The ellipsis itself takes 1 character.
    available = limit - 1  # reserve 1 char for '…'

    if available < 0:
        return ""
    if available == 0:
        return "…"

    # Truncate to `available` characters, then cut back to last word boundary
    truncated = text[:available]

    # If the next character in the original text is not a space, we cut mid-word;
    # find the last space in the truncated portion.
    if len(text) > available and text[available] != ' ':
        last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')
        if last_space != -1:
            truncated = truncated[:last_space]

    # Strip any trailing spaces
    truncated = truncated.rstrip()

    return truncated + '…'

def truncate_headline(text, limit):
    """Shorten a headline to fit within limit characters.

    Cuts on a word boundary and appends a single ellipsis character '…'.
    If the text already fits within limit, return it unchanged.
    """
    if len(text) <= limit:
        return text

    # The ellipsis takes 1 character, so we have (limit - 1) chars for text
    available = limit - 1

    if available <= 0:
        return "…"

    # Truncate at available characters
    truncated = text[:available]

    # Find the last word boundary (space)
    last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')

    if last_space > 0:
        truncated = truncated[:last_space]

    return truncated + '…'

import re


def parse_iso_date(s):
    """Parse an ISO date string like '2026-06-05' and return (year, month, day) as ints.

    Raises ValueError if the input is not a valid date.
    """
    if not isinstance(s, str):
        raise ValueError(f"Expected a string, got {type(s)}")

    pattern = r'^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$'
    if not re.match(pattern, s):
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid ISO date format: {s!r}")

    year_s, month_s, day_s = s.split('-')
    year = int(year_s)
    month = int(month_s)
    day = int(day_s)

    # Validate month range
    if not (1 <= month <= 12):
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid month: {month}")

    # Validate day range using days-per-month logic
    days_in_month = [0, 31, 28, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31]

    # Leap year check
    if (year % 4 == 0 and year % 100 != 0) or (year % 400 == 0):
        days_in_month[2] = 29

    if not (1 <= day <= days_in_month[month]):
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid day {day} for month {month} in year {year}")

    return (year, month, day)

import re


def parse_iso_date(s):
    """Parse an ISO date string like '2026-06-05' and return (year, month, day) as integers.

    Raises ValueError if the input is not a valid date.
    """
    if not isinstance(s, str):
        raise ValueError(f"Expected a string, got {type(s).__name__}")

    match = re.fullmatch(r'(\d{4})-(\d{2})-(\d{2})', s)
    if not match:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid ISO date format: {s!r}")

    year = int(match.group(1))
    month = int(match.group(2))
    day = int(match.group(3))

    # Validate ranges
    if month < 1 or month > 12:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid month: {month}")

    # Days per month (non-leap year)
    days_in_month = [0, 31, 28, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31]

    # Leap year check
    if (year % 4 == 0 and year % 100 != 0) or (year % 400 == 0):
        days_in_month[2] = 29

    if day < 1 or day > days_in_month[month]:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid day {day} for month {month} in year {year}")

    return (year, month, day)

from urllib.parse import urlparse


def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
    """Normalize a URL to a canonical form for deduplication."""
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url.strip().lower())

        # Normalize netloc - strip www. prefix
        netloc = parsed.netloc.lstrip("www.")

        # Normalize path - remove trailing slash
        path = parsed.path.rstrip("/") or "/"

        # Ignore query params and fragments: they often carry feed-specific
        # tracking info (utm_source, etc.) that differ across feeds for the
        # same story.
        return f"{netloc}{path}"
    except Exception:
        return url


def dedupe_articles(articles):
    """
    Remove duplicate articles from a list, keeping the first occurrence.

    The same story may arrive from different feeds with slightly different URLs.
    Deduplication is based on normalized URL comparison.
    """
    seen = set()
    result = []

    for article in articles:
        url = article.get("url", "")
        normalized = _normalize_url(url)

        if normalized not in seen:
            seen.add(normalized)
            result.append(article)

    return result

from urllib.parse import urlparse


def normalize_url(url):
    """Normalize a URL for deduplication purposes."""
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url.strip().lower())
        scheme = parsed.scheme or 'http'
        netloc = parsed.netloc
        if netloc.startswith('www.'):
            netloc = netloc[4:]
        path = parsed.path.rstrip('/')
        if not path:
            path = ''
        # Drop query params and fragments (same story, different tracking params)
        return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}"
    except Exception:
        return url.strip().lower()


def dedupe_articles(articles):
    """
    Remove duplicate articles, keeping the first occurrence.
    Deduplication is based on normalized URL to handle minor URL variations
    such as www prefix, trailing slashes, query parameters, and case differences.
    """
    seen = set()
    result = []
    for article in articles:
        url = article.get('url', '')
        key = normalize_url(url)
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            result.append(article)
    return result

from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlunparse, parse_qs, urlencode


def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
    """Normalize a URL to a canonical form for deduplication."""
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url.strip())
        # Lowercase scheme and host
        scheme = parsed.scheme.lower()
        netloc = parsed.netloc.lower()
        # Remove trailing slash from path unless it's just "/"
        path = parsed.path.rstrip("/") or "/"
        # Remove common tracking parameters
        _TRACKING_PARAMS = {
            "utm_source", "utm_medium", "utm_campaign", "utm_term", "utm_content",
            "ref", "referer", "source", "fbclid", "gclid", "mc_cid", "mc_eid",
            "yclid", "_ga", "igshid",
        }
        qs = parse_qs(parsed.query, keep_blank_values=True)
        filtered = {k: v for k, v in qs.items() if k.lower() not in _TRACKING_PARAMS}
        # Sort query params for stable comparison
        query = urlencode(sorted(filtered.items()))
        # Ignore fragment
        normalized = urlunparse((scheme, netloc, path, "", query, ""))
        return normalized
    except Exception:
        return url


def dedupe_articles(articles):
    """
    Remove duplicate articles, keeping the first occurrence.
    Deduplication is based on a normalized URL.
    """
    seen = set()
    result = []
    for article in articles:
        url = article.get("url", "")
        key = _normalize_url(url)
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            result.append(article)
    return result

"""
dedupe_articles module: removes duplicate articles from a list,
keeping the first occurrence. Handles slightly different URLs from
different feeds via URL normalization.
"""

from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlunparse, parse_qs, urlencode


def _normalize_url(url):
    """
    Normalize a URL for deduplication purposes:
    - Lowercase scheme and host
    - Remove trailing slashes from path
    - Remove common tracking query parameters (utm_*, fbclid, etc.)
    - Sort remaining query parameters for consistent comparison
    - Remove fragment
    """
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url.strip())
    except Exception:
        return url.strip().lower()

    scheme = parsed.scheme.lower()
    netloc = parsed.netloc.lower()
    path = parsed.path.rstrip('/')

    # Remove common tracking/noise query params
    TRACKING_PARAMS = {
        'utm_source', 'utm_medium', 'utm_campaign', 'utm_term', 'utm_content',
        'fbclid', 'gclid', 'msclkid', 'ref', 'source', 'via', 'feed',
        'from', 'cmpid', 'cmp', 'WT.mc_id', 'mc_cid', 'mc_eid',
    }

    raw_params = parse_qs(parsed.query, keep_blank_values=False)
    filtered = {
        k: v for k, v in raw_params.items()
        if k.lower() not in {p.lower() for p in TRACKING_PARAMS}
    }
    # Sort for stable comparison
    sorted_query = urlencode(sorted(filtered.items()), doseq=True)

    normalized = urlunparse((scheme, netloc, path, '', sorted_query, ''))
    return normalized


def dedupe_articles(articles):
    """
    Remove duplicate articles from a list, keeping the first occurrence.
    Two articles are considered duplicates if their normalized URLs match.
    """
    seen = set()
    result = []
    for article in articles:
        url = article.get('url', '')
        key = _normalize_url(url)
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            result.append(article)
    return result

from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlunparse, parse_qs, urlencode


def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
    """Normalize a URL for deduplication purposes."""
    url = url.strip()

    # Add scheme if missing so urlparse works correctly
    if not url.startswith(("http://", "https://")):
        url = "https://" + url

    parsed = urlparse(url)

    # Normalize scheme to https
    scheme = "https"

    # Normalize netloc: lowercase, strip www.
    netloc = parsed.netloc.lower()
    if netloc.startswith("www."):
        netloc = netloc[4:]

    # Normalize path: strip trailing slash (but keep root "/")
    path = parsed.path.rstrip("/") or "/"

    # Drop common tracking/utm query parameters; keep the rest sorted
    TRACKING_PARAMS = {
        "utm_source", "utm_medium", "utm_campaign", "utm_term", "utm_content",
        "ref", "source", "fbclid", "gclid", "mc_cid", "mc_eid",
        "_ga", "yclid",
    }
    qs = parse_qs(parsed.query, keep_blank_values=True)
    filtered_qs = {k: v for k, v in qs.items() if k.lower() not in TRACKING_PARAMS}
    query = urlencode(sorted(filtered_qs.items()))

    # Drop fragment entirely
    normalized = urlunparse((scheme, netloc, path, "", query, ""))
    return normalized


def dedupe_articles(articles):
    """
    Remove duplicate articles, keeping the first occurrence.

    Two articles are considered duplicates if their URLs normalize to the
    same canonical form (same host, path, and non-tracking query params,
    ignoring scheme differences and www prefix).
    """
    seen = set()
    result = []
    for article in articles:
        url = article.get("url", "")
        key = _normalize_url(url)
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            result.append(article)
    return result

from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs, urlencode, urlunparse


def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
    """Normalize a URL to a canonical form for deduplication."""
    url = url.strip()

    # Parse the URL
    parsed = urlparse(url)

    # Normalize scheme: treat http and https as the same (use https)
    scheme = "https" if parsed.scheme.lower() in ("http", "https") else parsed.scheme.lower()

    # Normalize netloc: lowercase, strip www.
    netloc = parsed.netloc.lower()
    if netloc.startswith("www."):
        netloc = netloc[4:]

    # Normalize path: remove trailing slash (unless it's just "/")
    path = parsed.path
    if path != "/" and path.endswith("/"):
        path = path.rstrip("/")

    # Normalize query: sort params, drop tracking params
    TRACKING_PARAMS = {
        "utm_source", "utm_medium", "utm_campaign", "utm_term", "utm_content",
        "fbclid", "gclid", "ref", "source", "campaign",
    }
    if parsed.query:
        params = parse_qs(parsed.query, keep_blank_values=True)
        # Remove tracking params
        params = {k: v for k, v in params.items() if k.lower() not in TRACKING_PARAMS}
        # Sort for consistency
        query = urlencode(sorted(params.items()))
    else:
        query = ""

    # Rebuild without fragment
    normalized = urlunparse((scheme, netloc, path, parsed.params, query, ""))
    return normalized


def dedupe_articles(articles):
    """
    Remove duplicate articles, keeping the first occurrence.
    Two articles are considered duplicates if their normalized URLs match.
    """
    seen = set()
    result = []
    for article in articles:
        url = article.get("url", "")
        key = _normalize_url(url)
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            result.append(article)
    return result

from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlunparse, urlencode, parse_qs


def normalize_url(url):
    """Normalize a URL to a canonical form for deduplication."""
    url = url.strip()

    parsed = urlparse(url)

    scheme = parsed.scheme.lower()
    netloc = parsed.netloc.lower()

    # Remove www. prefix so www.example.com and example.com match
    if netloc.startswith('www.'):
        netloc = netloc[4:]

    # Remove trailing slash (unless it's just '/')
    path = parsed.path
    if path != '/' and path.endswith('/'):
        path = path.rstrip('/')

    # Strip common tracking/campaign query params that vary by feed
    IGNORED_PARAMS = {
        'utm_source', 'utm_medium', 'utm_campaign', 'utm_term', 'utm_content',
        'utm_id', 'fbclid', 'gclid', 'ref', 'source', 'origin',
        'campaign', 'medium', 'referrer', 'via', 'trk',
    }

    if parsed.query:
        qs = parse_qs(parsed.query, keep_blank_values=True)
        qs = {k: v for k, v in qs.items() if k.lower() not in IGNORED_PARAMS}
        query = urlencode(sorted(qs.items()), doseq=True) if qs else ''
    else:
        query = ''

    # Drop fragment — anchors don't change the story identity
    normalized = urlunparse((scheme, netloc, path, parsed.params, query, ''))
    return normalized


def dedupe_articles(articles):
    """
    Remove duplicate articles from a list, keeping the first occurrence.

    The same story may arrive from different feeds with slightly different URLs
    (trailing slashes, www prefix, UTM tracking params, etc.).  URLs are
    normalized before comparing so those cosmetic variations are treated as the
    same story.

    Args:
        articles: list of dicts, each containing at least a ``"url"`` key.

    Returns:
        A new list with duplicates removed, preserving the original order of
        first occurrences.
    """
    seen = {}
    result = []
    for article in articles:
        url = article.get('url', '')
        key = normalize_url(url)
        if key not in seen:
            seen[key] = True
            result.append(article)
    return result

from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlencode, parse_qs, urlunparse


def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
    """Normalize a URL to a canonical form for deduplication."""
    url = url.strip()

    # Parse the URL
    parsed = urlparse(url)

    # Normalize scheme: treat http and https as equivalent (canonical: https)
    scheme = "https"

    # Normalize host to lowercase, strip www.
    host = parsed.netloc.lower()
    # Remove default ports
    if host.endswith(":80") or host.endswith(":443"):
        host = host.rsplit(":", 1)[0]
    # Strip leading www.
    if host.startswith("www."):
        host = host[4:]

    # Normalize path: remove trailing slash (unless it's just "/")
    path = parsed.path
    if path != "/" and path.endswith("/"):
        path = path.rstrip("/")

    # Filter out known tracking query parameters
    TRACKING_PARAMS = {
        "utm_source", "utm_medium", "utm_campaign", "utm_term", "utm_content",
        "utm_id", "utm_reader", "utm_name",
        "fbclid", "gclid", "msclkid", "dclid",
        "ref", "source", "feed", "from",
        "_ga", "_gl",
    }

    qs = parse_qs(parsed.query, keep_blank_values=False)
    filtered_qs = {k: v for k, v in qs.items() if k.lower() not in TRACKING_PARAMS}
    # Sort query params for consistent ordering
    sorted_qs = urlencode(sorted(filtered_qs.items()), doseq=True)

    # Drop fragment (anchors don't affect content identity)
    fragment = ""

    normalized = urlunparse((scheme, host, path, parsed.params, sorted_qs, fragment))
    return normalized


def dedupe_articles(articles: list) -> list:
    """
    Return articles with duplicate stories removed, keeping the first occurrence.

    Articles with the same normalized URL are considered duplicates.
    Normalization handles: http/https, www prefix, trailing slashes,
    tracking query parameters, fragments, and case differences.
    """
    seen = set()
    result = []
    for article in articles:
        url = article.get("url", "")
        key = _normalize_url(url)
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            result.append(article)
    return result

from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs, urlencode, urlunparse
import re


_TRACKING_PARAMS = {
    "utm_source", "utm_medium", "utm_campaign", "utm_term", "utm_content",
    "fbclid", "gclid", "msclkid", "ref", "referrer", "source", "campaign",
    "mc_cid", "mc_eid", "_ga", "yclid", "igshid",
}


def _normalize_url(url) -> str:
    """
    Normalize a URL to a canonical form for deduplication.

    Handles:
    - None / missing url  -> empty string (all missing-url articles share one key)
    - scheme case + http/https treated as equivalent
    - www. prefix removed
    - trailing slash removed
    - URL fragment removed (client-side only)
    - common tracking query parameters stripped
    - query parameter order normalized
    - overall case folded to lowercase
    """
    if url is None:
        return ''
    url = str(url).strip()
    if not url:
        return ''

    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url)
    except Exception:
        return url.lower()

    # Treat http and https as the same story
    scheme = parsed.scheme.lower()
    if scheme in ('http', 'https'):
        scheme = 'https'

    # Lowercase host, strip www.
    netloc = parsed.netloc.lower()
    netloc = re.sub(r'^www\.', '', netloc)

    # Remove trailing slash from path (keep root "/" as-is)
    path = parsed.path
    if path != '/' and path.endswith('/'):
        path = path.rstrip('/')

    # Strip tracking params; sort remaining for stable key
    if parsed.query:
        qs = parse_qs(parsed.query, keep_blank_values=True)
        qs_clean = {k: v for k, v in qs.items() if k.lower() not in _TRACKING_PARAMS}
        query = urlencode(sorted(qs_clean.items()))
    else:
        query = ''

    # Drop fragment
    normalized = urlunparse((scheme, netloc, path, parsed.params, query, ''))
    return normalized.lower()


def dedupe_articles(articles):
    """
    Return articles with duplicates removed, keeping the first occurrence.

    Two articles are considered duplicates when their normalized URLs match.
    Normalization handles minor URL variations that commonly appear when the
    same story arrives from different feeds (www prefix, trailing slash,
    http/https, UTM tracking parameters, URL fragments).
    """
    seen = set()
    result = []
    for article in articles:
        url = article.get('url')
        key = _normalize_url(url)
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            result.append(article)
    return result

import re
import unicodedata


def slugify(title):
    """
    Convert a title to a lowercase, hyphen-separated URL slug.
    Collapses runs of separators and trims leading/trailing hyphens.
    """
    # Normalize unicode characters (e.g., accented chars -> ASCII equivalents)
    value = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", str(title))
    value = value.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")

    # Lowercase
    value = value.lower()

    # Replace any non-alphanumeric characters (except hyphens) with hyphens
    value = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", value)

    # Trim leading and trailing hyphens
    value = value.strip("-")

    return value

import re
import unicodedata


def slugify(title):
    """Return a lowercase, hyphen-separated slug suitable for use in a URL.

    Collapses runs of separators and trims any leading or trailing hyphens.
    """
    # Normalize unicode characters to ASCII equivalents where possible
    value = unicodedata.normalize('NFKD', title)
    value = value.encode('ascii', 'ignore').decode('ascii')

    # Convert to lowercase
    value = value.lower()

    # Replace any non-alphanumeric character (except hyphens) with a hyphen
    value = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '-', value)

    # Strip leading and trailing hyphens
    value = value.strip('-')

    return value

import re
import unicodedata


def slugify(title):
    # Normalize unicode characters to ASCII
    value = unicodedata.normalize('NFKD', title)
    value = value.encode('ascii', 'ignore').decode('ascii')
    # Lowercase
    value = value.lower()
    # Replace non-alphanumeric characters (except hyphens) with hyphens
    value = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', value)
    # Replace whitespace and underscores with hyphens
    value = re.sub(r'[\s_]+', '-', value)
    # Collapse runs of hyphens
    value = re.sub(r'-+', '-', value)
    # Trim leading and trailing hyphens
    value = value.strip('-')
    return value

import re
import unicodedata


def slugify(title):
    """
    Convert an article title into a lowercase, hyphen-separated URL slug.
    Collapses runs of separators and trims leading/trailing hyphens.
    """
    # Normalize unicode characters (NFKD decomposition)
    value = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
    # Encode to ASCII bytes, ignoring non-ASCII, then decode back
    value = value.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")
    # Lowercase
    value = value.lower()
    # Replace any non-alphanumeric characters with hyphens
    value = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", value)
    # Strip leading/trailing hyphens
    value = value.strip("-")
    return value

import re
import unicodedata


def slugify(title: str) -> str:
    """Return a lowercase, hyphen-separated slug suitable for use in a URL.

    Steps:
    1. Normalize unicode characters (NFKD) and encode to ASCII, dropping
       characters that can't be represented.
    2. Lowercase.
    3. Replace any character that is not alphanumeric with a hyphen,
       collapsing runs of non-alphanumeric characters into a single hyphen.
    4. Strip leading/trailing hyphens.
    """
    # Normalize unicode to ASCII, drop non-ASCII
    normalized = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
    ascii_text = normalized.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")

    # Lowercase
    lowered = ascii_text.lower()

    # Replace non-alphanumeric characters with hyphens (collapses runs too)
    replaced = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", lowered)

    # Strip leading/trailing hyphens
    stripped = replaced.strip("-")

    return stripped

import re
import unicodedata


def slugify(title: str) -> str:
    """Return a lowercase, hyphen-separated slug suitable for use in a URL.

    Steps:
    1. Normalize unicode to ASCII (NFKD then encode/decode to strip accents).
    2. Lowercase.
    3. Replace any character that is not alphanumeric or a hyphen with a hyphen.
    4. Collapse runs of hyphens into a single hyphen.
    5. Strip leading and trailing hyphens.
    """
    # Normalize unicode characters to their closest ASCII equivalent
    normalized = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
    ascii_text = normalized.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")

    # Lowercase
    lower = ascii_text.lower()

    # Replace non-alphanumeric characters with hyphens (also collapses runs)
    replaced = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", lower)

    # Strip leading/trailing hyphens
    slug = replaced.strip("-")

    return slug

import re


def slugify(title: str) -> str:
    """Convert an article title into a lowercase, hyphen-separated URL slug."""
    # Lowercase
    slug = title.lower()
    # Replace any non-alphanumeric characters (separators, punctuation, whitespace) with hyphens
    slug = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '-', slug)
    # Strip leading and trailing hyphens
    slug = slug.strip('-')
    return slug

import re


def slugify(title):
    """Convert an article title to a lowercase, hyphen-separated URL slug.

    Collapses runs of separators and trims leading/trailing hyphens.
    """
    slug = title.lower()
    slug = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '-', slug)
    slug = slug.strip('-')
    return slug

import re
import unicodedata


def slugify(title: str) -> str:
    """
    Convert an article title to a URL-friendly slug.

    - Lowercase
    - Non-alphanumeric characters replaced with hyphens
    - Runs of separators collapsed to a single hyphen
    - Leading and trailing hyphens trimmed
    """
    # Normalize unicode characters (e.g. accented letters -> base letters)
    normalized = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
    # Encode to ASCII, ignoring characters that can't be converted
    ascii_str = normalized.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")
    # Lowercase
    lower = ascii_str.lower()
    # Replace any non-alphanumeric character with a hyphen
    hyphenated = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", lower)
    # Trim leading/trailing hyphens
    slug = hyphenated.strip("-")
    return slug

def fit_to_budget(text, budget=280):
    if len(text) <= budget:
        return text
    # Truncate at a word boundary
    truncated = text[:budget]
    # Find the last space within the budget
    last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')
    if last_space == -1:
        # No space found; single word longer than budget, must hard-cut
        return truncated
    return truncated[:last_space]

def fit_to_budget(text, budget=280):
    """Shorten text to fit within budget characters without cutting a word in half."""
    if len(text) <= budget:
        return text

    # Truncate to budget characters, then cut back to last complete word
    truncated = text[:budget]

    # Find the last space to avoid cutting a word in half
    last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')

    if last_space == -1:
        # No space found - single word longer than budget, must cut it
        return truncated

    return truncated[:last_space]

def fit_to_budget(text, budget=280):
    """Shorten text to fit within budget characters without cutting a word in half.

    If the text already fits within the budget, it is returned unchanged.
    Otherwise, the text is truncated at the last complete word boundary that
    keeps the result within the budget. If no complete word fits, returns an
    empty string.
    """
    if len(text) <= budget:
        return text

    truncated = text[:budget]

    # Check whether the truncation landed inside a word
    if text[budget] != ' ':
        # We cut mid-word; walk back to the last space
        last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')
        if last_space == -1:
            # The entire budget is one partial word; nothing complete fits
            return ''
        return truncated[:last_space]
    else:
        # The truncation landed exactly on a space; strip any trailing spaces
        return truncated.rstrip()

def fit_to_budget(text, budget=280):
    if len(text) <= budget:
        return text
    # We need to cut at or before `budget` without splitting a word.
    truncated = text[:budget]
    # If the character right after the cut is a space, no word was split.
    if budget < len(text) and text[budget] == ' ':
        return truncated
    # Otherwise find the last space in the truncated string to avoid cutting a word.
    last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')
    if last_space == -1:
        # No spaces; the whole truncated string is one word, return it as-is.
        return truncated
    return truncated[:last_space]

def fit_to_budget(text, budget=280):
    if len(text) <= budget:
        return text
    # Truncate to budget characters first.
    truncated = text[:budget]
    # If the character right after the cut is a space, no word was split.
    if budget < len(text) and text[budget] == ' ':
        return truncated
    # Otherwise find the last space in the truncated string to avoid splitting a word.
    last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')
    if last_space == -1:
        # No spaces found; the whole truncated string is one long word, return it as-is.
        return truncated
    return truncated[:last_space]

def fit_to_budget(text, budget=280):
    """Shorten text to fit within budget characters without cutting a word in half.

    If the text already fits within budget, return it unchanged.
    Otherwise, truncate at the last word boundary at or before budget characters.
    """
    if len(text) <= budget:
        return text

    # text[budget] exists because len(text) > budget
    # If the character at position budget is a space, text[:budget] ends a complete word
    if text[budget] == ' ':
        return text[:budget]

    # text[:budget] would end in the middle of a word; find the last space before it
    truncated = text[:budget]
    last_space = truncated.rfind(' ')

    if last_space == -1:
        # No word boundary found: single token longer than budget
        return ''

    return text[:last_space]

def rank_by_recency(articles):
    return sorted(articles, key=lambda a: a.get("ts", float("-inf")), reverse=True)

def rank_by_recency(articles):
    return sorted(articles, key=lambda a: a.get("ts", float("-inf")), reverse=True)

def rank_by_recency(articles):
    return sorted(articles, key=lambda a: a.get("ts", 0) or 0, reverse=True)

from urllib.parse import urlparse

# Common multi-part TLDs (public suffix list subset)
# These are TLDs that have more than one component
MULTI_PART_TLDS = {
    # UK
    "co.uk", "org.uk", "me.uk", "net.uk", "ltd.uk", "plc.uk", "gov.uk", "sch.uk",
    # Australia
    "com.au", "net.au", "org.au", "edu.au", "gov.au", "asn.au", "id.au",
    # Other common ones
    "co.nz", "org.nz", "net.nz", "gov.nz",
    "co.jp", "or.jp", "ne.jp", "go.jp",
    "co.in", "net.in", "org.in", "gov.in",
    "co.za", "org.za", "net.za",
    "co.kr", "or.kr", "ne.kr",
    "com.br", "net.br", "org.br", "gov.br",
    "com.mx", "net.mx", "org.mx",
    "com.ar", "net.ar", "org.ar",
    "com.cn", "net.cn", "org.cn", "gov.cn",
    "com.sg", "net.sg", "org.sg",
    "com.hk", "net.hk", "org.hk",
    "com.tw", "net.tw", "org.tw",
    "com.de", "com.fr",
    "co.il", "org.il", "net.il",
    "co.ke", "or.ke",
    "co.tz", "or.tz",
    "co.ug", "or.ug",
    "co.zw", "org.zw",
    "com.ng", "org.ng", "net.ng",
    "com.gh", "org.gh",
    "com.pk", "net.pk", "org.pk",
}


def extract_domain(url: str) -> str:
    """Return the registrable (base) domain from a URL, lowercased.

    Strips the scheme, any userinfo and port, the path and query, and any
    subdomains, returning only the registrable domain lowercased.
    """
    # Parse the URL to get the hostname
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    hostname = parsed.hostname or ""

    # If parsing didn't work well (e.g., URL without scheme), try adding a scheme
    if not hostname and url:
        try:
            parsed = urlparse("http://" + url)
            hostname = parsed.hostname or ""
        except Exception:
            hostname = ""

    hostname = hostname.lower()

    parts = hostname.split(".")

    if len(parts) < 2:
        return hostname

    # Check if last two parts form a known multi-part TLD
    if len(parts) >= 3:
        possible_two_part_tld = ".".join(parts[-2:])
        if possible_two_part_tld in MULTI_PART_TLDS:
            # Registrable domain = last 3 parts
            return ".".join(parts[-3:])

    # Default: registrable domain = last 2 parts
    return ".".join(parts[-2:])

from urllib.parse import urlparse


def extract_domain(url: str) -> str:
    """
    Extract the registrable (base) domain from a URL.
    Strips scheme, userinfo, port, path, query, and subdomains.
    Returns the result lowercased.
    """
    # Normalize and parse; prepend scheme if missing so urlparse handles it correctly
    url_lower = url.lower()
    if not (url_lower.startswith("http://") or url_lower.startswith("https://")
            or url_lower.startswith("ftp://") or url_lower.startswith("ftps://")
            or url_lower.startswith("//")):
        url = "http://" + url

    parsed = urlparse(url)
    hostname = (parsed.hostname or "").lower()

    # Split by dot and get the last two parts (SLD + TLD)
    parts = [p for p in hostname.split(".") if p]

    if len(parts) >= 2:
        return ".".join(parts[-2:])

    return hostname

def extract_domain(url: str) -> str:
    """Return the registrable (base) domain from a URL, lowercased.

    Strips the scheme, userinfo, port, path, query, and any subdomains,
    returning only the registrable domain (e.g. 'example.com' or 'bbc.co.uk').
    """
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    hostname = parsed.hostname or ""

    # Handle scheme-less URLs like 'example.com/path'
    if not hostname and url:
        try:
            parsed = urlparse("http://" + url)
            hostname = parsed.hostname or ""
        except Exception:
            hostname = ""

    hostname = hostname.lower()
    parts = hostname.split(".")

    if len(parts) < 2:
        # Single label (e.g. 'localhost') — return as-is
        return hostname

    # If the last two labels form a known multi-part TLD, the registrable
    # domain is the last three labels (e.g. 'bbc.co.uk').
    if len(parts) >= 3:
        candidate_tld = ".".join(parts[-2:])
        if candidate_tld in MULTI_PART_TLDS:
            return ".".join(parts[-3:])

    # Default: registrable domain is the last two labels (e.g. 'example.com').
    return ".".join(parts[-2:])

"""
extract_domain: returns the registrable (base) domain from a URL.
Strips scheme, userinfo, port, path, query, fragment, and subdomains.
Returns result lowercased.
"""

import re
from urllib.parse import urlparse


def _is_ip_address(hostname: str) -> bool:
    """Return True if hostname is an IPv4 or IPv6 address."""
    ipv4_pattern = re.compile(r"^(\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}$")
    if ipv4_pattern.match(hostname):
        return True
    if ":" in hostname:
        return True
    return False


def extract_domain(url: str) -> str:
    """Return the registrable (base) domain from a URL.

    Strips the scheme, any userinfo and port, the path and query,
    and any subdomains. Returns the result lowercased.
    """
    if "://" not in url:
        url = "http://" + url

    parsed = urlparse(url)
    hostname = parsed.hostname or ""
    hostname = hostname.lower()

    if hostname.endswith("."):
        hostname = hostname[:-1]

    if _is_ip_address(hostname):
        return hostname

    parts = hostname.split(".")

    MULTI_PART_TLDS = {
        "co.uk", "org.uk", "me.uk", "net.uk", "ltd.uk", "plc.uk",
        "co.au", "com.au", "net.au", "org.au", "edu.au", "gov.au",
        "co.nz", "com.nz", "net.nz", "org.nz",
        "co.in", "com.in", "net.in", "org.in", "gov.in",
        "co.jp", "com.jp", "ne.jp", "or.jp", "go.jp",
        "co.za", "org.za", "net.za",
        "co.ke", "or.ke",
        "co.il", "org.il",
        "co.kr", "or.kr",
        "com.br", "net.br", "org.br", "gov.br",
        "com.mx", "net.mx", "org.mx",
        "com.ar", "net.ar", "org.ar",
        "com.cn", "net.cn", "org.cn", "gov.cn",
        "com.sg", "net.sg", "org.sg", "gov.sg",
        "com.hk", "net.hk", "org.hk",
        "com.tw", "net.tw", "org.tw",
        "com.tr", "net.tr", "org.tr",
        "com.my", "net.my", "org.my",
        "com.ph", "net.ph", "org.ph",
        "com.pk", "net.pk", "org.pk",
        "com.ng", "net.ng", "org.ng",
        "ac.uk", "gov.uk",
        "ac.nz", "ac.in", "ac.jp", "ac.za",
    }

    if len(parts) >= 3:
        potential_tld = ".".join(parts[-2:])
        if potential_tld in MULTI_PART_TLDS:
            return ".".join(parts[-3:])

    if len(parts) >= 2:
        return ".".join(parts[-2:])

    return hostname

"""
extract_domain: returns the registrable (base) domain from a URL.
"""
import re
from urllib.parse import urlparse

try:
    import tldextract as _tldextract
    _HAS_TLDEXTRACT = True
except ImportError:
    _HAS_TLDEXTRACT = False


# Known multi-part TLDs for the fallback path (non-exhaustive but covers common cases)
_MULTI_PART_TLDS = {
    "co.uk", "co.in", "co.jp", "co.nz", "co.za", "co.kr", "co.id",
    "co.il", "co.th", "co.tz", "co.ug", "co.ke",
    "com.au", "com.br", "com.cn", "com.mx", "com.sg", "com.tw", "com.hk",
    "com.ar", "com.pe", "com.co", "com.ng", "com.ph",
    "org.uk", "net.uk", "me.uk", "ltd.uk", "plc.uk",
    "org.au", "net.au", "id.au", "gov.au",
    "ac.uk", "gov.uk", "sch.uk",
    "ne.jp", "ac.jp", "go.jp", "or.jp",
}


def _fallback_extract(hostname: str) -> str:
    """Fallback when tldextract is unavailable. Handles common multi-part TLDs."""
    parts = hostname.split(".")
    if len(parts) <= 2:
        return hostname
    # Check if last two parts form a known multi-part TLD
    candidate_tld = ".".join(parts[-2:])
    if candidate_tld in _MULTI_PART_TLDS and len(parts) >= 3:
        # registrable = last 3 parts
        return ".".join(parts[-3:])
    # Default: last 2 parts
    return ".".join(parts[-2:])


def extract_domain(url: str) -> str:
    """
    Return the registrable (base) domain from a URL.

    Strips scheme, userinfo, port, path, query, fragment, and any subdomains.
    Returns the result lowercased.

    Examples:
        extract_domain("https://www.example.com/path") -> "example.com"
        extract_domain("https://news.bbc.co.uk/article") -> "bbc.co.uk"
        extract_domain("ftp://user:pass@files.example.org:21/") -> "example.org"
    """
    # Prepend a dummy scheme so urlparse handles bare hostnames correctly
    if "://" not in url:
        url = "http://" + url

    parsed = urlparse(url)
    hostname = (parsed.hostname or "").lower()

    if not hostname:
        return ""

    # Return IP addresses unchanged
    if re.match(r'^\d{1,3}(\.\d{1,3}){3}$', hostname):
        return hostname

    # IPv6 addresses (urlparse strips the brackets)
    if ":" in hostname:
        return hostname

    if _HAS_TLDEXTRACT:
        extracted = _tldextract.extract(hostname)
        if extracted.domain and extracted.suffix:
            return f"{extracted.domain}.{extracted.suffix}"
        if extracted.domain:
            return extracted.domain
        return hostname

    return _fallback_extract(hostname)

def merge_translations(base, overrides):
    result = dict(base)
    result.update(overrides)
    return result
